skill.name: backend-api
skill.description: Design and implement RESTful API endpoints with guidance for routing, controllers, versioning, query parameters, status codes, and rate limiting.
skill.summary: Guide REST API endpoint design and implementation with practical backend conventions.
skill.author: DevanB

security_audit.summary: Static shell-execution and weak-cryptography detections are false positives from markdown code spans, API terminology, and HTTP examples in SKILL.md. The only retained concern is a parent-directory documentation link at SKILL.md line 27, which is not executable but can direct the agent outside the packaged skill. No evidence of prompt injection, network access, credential access, or malicious behavior was found.

low_findings.title: Parent-Directory Documentation Reference
low_findings.description: SKILL.md links to ../../../agent-os/standards/backend/api.md. This is not executable path traversal, but it can make the skill depend on files outside the packaged skill boundary.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The parent-directory path is directly present in the skill file. Risk is limited because it is a markdown link, not code that reads or writes files.

content.value_statement: Backend API work often fails when routes, controllers, and response rules are inconsistent. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through REST endpoint design using clear HTTP conventions.
actual_capabilities: Guides REST route design for resource-based endpoints.
Helps choose HTTP methods for create, read, update, and delete workflows.
Supports API versioning decisions using URL paths or headers.
Provides direction for filtering, sorting, pagination, and search parameters.
Guides response status code choices for common API outcomes.
Covers rate limiting and throttling considerations for API endpoints.
limitations: The referenced backend API standard file is outside this skill package.
It provides guidance only and does not include executable generators or tests.
Framework-specific implementation details may need project inspection.
It does not define authentication, authorization, or data validation rules in detail.

use_cases.title: Create a New Endpoint
use_cases.description: Plan route names, controller actions, request methods, and response codes for a new REST resource.

use_cases.title: Review API Consistency
use_cases.description: Check an existing API surface for inconsistent paths, methods, versioning, or query parameter behavior.

use_cases.title: Standardize Controller Work
use_cases.description: Apply common API conventions when modifying Laravel routes, controllers, resources, and response formatting.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Basic Endpoint
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single REST resource.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the backend-api skill to design a REST endpoint for this resource. Include route path, HTTP method, controller action, expected status codes, and query parameters.

prompt_templates.title: Update an Existing Route
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for changing current API behavior.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the backend-api skill to review these existing route and controller files. Recommend the smallest changes needed for RESTful naming, methods, status codes, and pagination.

prompt_templates.title: Plan API Versioning
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the backend-api skill to compare URL path versioning and header-based versioning for this API. Recommend one approach and list required route, controller, and client changes.

prompt_templates.title: Audit an API Surface
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a broad design review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the backend-api skill to audit this API module for route structure, nested resources, query parameters, rate limiting, response formatting, and HTTP status code consistency.

output_examples.input: Design an endpoint for listing customer invoices with search and pagination.
output_examples.output: A concise endpoint plan with the collection route, GET method, supported search and pagination parameters, success and error status codes, and controller responsibility notes.

output_examples.input: Review these API routes for REST consistency.
output: A short list of route naming issues.
Status code corrections for create, update, delete, and not found responses.

output_examples.input: Add versioning guidance for a public API.
output_examples.output: A recommendation for versioning style, migration steps for existing clients, and notes about route grouping and response compatibility.

best_practices: Inspect the existing routing and controller patterns before adding new endpoints.
Keep endpoint paths resource-based and use HTTP methods to express actions.
Document expected status codes, query parameters, and throttling behavior with each change.
anti_patterns: Do not create action-heavy paths when a resource path and HTTP method can express the behavior.
Do not mix pagination, filtering, and sorting parameter names across related endpoints.
Do not return generic success responses when a specific HTTP status code is more accurate.

faq.question: Does this skill generate backend code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance that an AI coding tool can apply while editing project files.

faq.question: Which frameworks does it target?
faq.answer: The examples reference Laravel-style routes and controllers, but the REST guidance is broadly useful.

faq.question: Can it help with API versioning?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers URL path versioning and header-based versioning decisions.

faq.question: Does it include authentication guidance?
faq.answer: Only indirectly. Authentication and authorization rules should be handled by project-specific security standards.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a low-risk note about a parent-directory documentation link outside the skill package.

faq.question: Why were static shell findings dismissed?
faq.answer: The cited lines contain markdown inline code for paths and route examples, not commands that execute a shell.
